prompt: I have a personal website at example.com hosted on Nginx. My Nostr public key is b0635d6a9851d3aed0cd6c495b282167acf761729078d975fc341b22650b07b9. I want to set up NIP-05 verification so people can find me as bob@example.com. Walk me through the complete setup — the nostr.json file, the Nginx config, and what I need to update in my Nostr profile.
assertions: The hex pubkey b0635d6a9851d3aed0cd6c495b282167acf761729078d975fc341b22650b07b9 is present in the output
The word hex appears in the context of pubkey format requirements
Access-Control-Allow-Origin is set in the server configuration
The path /.well-known/nostr.json appears in a server location block
HTTPS is stated as a requirement for the nostr.json endpoint
The nip05 field value bob@example.com appears in a profile update
A curl command is used to test or verify the endpoint
Redirects are warned against or prohibited for the nostr.json endpoint

prompt: I'm building a NIP-05 identity provider service. I need a dynamic Node.js server that handles the /.well-known/nostr.json endpoint for multiple users. It should support the ?name= query parameter, include relay hints in the response, validate the local-part format, and handle the root identifier (_@domain). Give me the complete server code and explain the nostr.json response format.
assertions: The path /.well-known/nostr.json appears in a route handler
The name query parameter is extracted from the request
The JSON response has a names object and a relays object
Relay values are arrays with wss:// WebSocket URLs
Access-Control-Allow-Origin header is set on responses
Input validation restricts the local-part to allowed characters
The underscore _ is handled as the root or domain identifier
Example pubkeys are 64-character lowercase hex strings

prompt: My NIP-05 verification isn't working. I set nip05 to 'Alice@MyDomain.com' in my profile. My nostr.json file at https://mydomain.com/.well-known/nostr.json contains: {"names":{"Alice":"npub1dp4rcz7ufm4rrequena8s8nh5aykdw9t7e2dj0gya9j9sskjhk0qdjd0lv"}}. When I curl the endpoint, I get a 301 redirect to https://www.mydomain.com/.well-known/nostr.json. What's wrong and how do I fix everything?
assertions: Alice must be changed to lowercase alice
The npub format is identified as incorrect and hex format is required
The 301 redirect is identified as a NIP-05 spec violation
A corrected nostr.json with alice in lowercase and a hex pubkey is provided
The corrected nip05 profile value alice@mydomain.com appears in lowercase
Server configuration guidance for eliminating the redirect is given
CORS headers (Access-Control-Allow-Origin) are mentioned as required
Hex pubkeys are described as 64 characters long
